desc: I am a sleepy coder.
I commit in the morning.
I commit in the evening.
I pushed a commit with "dead" 2 times.
When I delete code, I delete a lot.
When I delete code, I delete a lot.
I did 2 sequential fixes.
body: Commits: - de668b5: Fixed unit tests - 089809d: Fixed unit tests

desc: I did 6 sequential fixes.
body: Commits: - 51cdf11: Fixes #9 - cc4585b: Fixed missing imports - c9c945c: Fixed missing imports - 2f0d13e: Fixed missing imports - 71b10f8: Fixed wrong branch name in workflow - ffcb938: Fixed missing imports

desc: I did 3 sequential fixes.
body: Commits: - 9ca71d9: fix missing newlines between groups in usage using multiple groups, fixes #15 - ef3ecaf: Fixed double newline in usage when no arguments exists in default group and arguments are in other groups, fixes #14 - 093bcfc: Fixed no alphabetical order in help message printing subparser names, fixes #13

desc: I did 4 sequential fixes.
body: Commits: - 8080f98: Fix NtSecurityDescriptor.Unmarshal returning inflated RawBytesSize Components (Owner, Group, DACL, SACL) are at specific offsets within the buffer, not laid out sequentially. Summing their sizes produced a value larger than the actual descriptor. Now tracks the maximum extent (offset + component size) to return the correct total size. - 063a907: Fix GUID FromFormatX incorrectly parsing D and E fields The D field was parsed from only one byte (parts[3]) instead of two bytes (parts[3] and parts[4]). The E field loop started at parts[3] instead of parts[5], overlapping with D and producing an 8-byte value instead of 6. Now correctly constructs D from 2 bytes and E from 6. - 6a2e612: Fix SID.Marshal dropping trailing zero sub-authorities Marshal() was skipping trailing zero-valued sub-authorities, which corrupts SIDs that legitimately contain zeros (e.g. domain SIDs with zero sub-authority components). All sub-authorities are now serialized regardless of their value. - fbb01ed: Fix SID.FromString setting wrong SubAuthorityCount for single-subauthority SIDs When a SID had exactly one sub-authority (e.g. S-1-1-0, S-1-5-18), SubAuthorityCount was left at 0 instead of being set to 1. The binary SID format counts the RID as a sub-authority, so Marshal() on such a SID would produce an incorrect binary representation with no RID bytes.

desc: I did 7 sequential fixes.
body: Commits: - 4be89f4: Fixed E712 comparison to True should be 'if cond is True:' or 'if cond:' - 2ccc492: Fixed W605 invalid escape sequence - 9031e12: Fixed W293 blank line contains whitespace - 5ecbdea: Fixed E303 too many blank lines - 7d3fdd6: Fixed W291 trailing whitespace - 8c79948: Fixed C901 too complex - 53272a2: Fixed F401 unused imports

desc: I found the answer to the ultimate question of life, the universe, and everything!
My favorite word is "added".
body:
My favorite commit message words are:

1. added (used 2160 times)
2. kernel (used 1205 times)
3. update (used 784 times)
4. readme.md (used 658 times)
5. symbols (used 637 times)
